1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL THIS earth to witness --

1.author: Arberry
1.text: No! I swear by this land,

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Nay! I swear by this city

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Nay! I swear by this city;

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Nay, I swear by this city -

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: No, I swear by this country (Mecca),

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: I swear by this city, Makkah -

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: I do not (need to) swear by this town (Mecca)

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Nay! I swear by this city.

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: I do call to witness this City;-

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: And you are free to live upon it, --

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: For you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) are in this city.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: and thou art a lodger in this land;

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: And thou shalt be allowed in yonder city -

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And you are free (from sin, to punish the enemies of Islam on the Day of the conquest) in this city (Makkah).

2.author: Itani
2.text: And you are a resident of this land.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: – this city wherein you have been rendered violable–

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: And you are free in this city.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: And thou art an indweller of this city -

2.author: Qarai
2.text: as you reside in this town;

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: and you are a lodger in this country.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And you, [O Muhammad], are free of restriction in this city -

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: in which you are now living

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And you shall be made free from obligation in this city--

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: and you are dwelling in this city --

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: And thou art a freeman of this City;-

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And the parent and the offspring,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And by oath of your forefather Ibrahim, and by you – his illustrious son!

3.author: Arberry
3.text: by the begetter, and that he begot,

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And by the begetter and that which he begat,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And by the begetter (i.e. Adam) and that which he begot (i.e. his progeny);

3.author: Itani
3.text: And by a father and what he fathered.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and I swear by the parent and his offspring:

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And by the begetter and that which he begot.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: And the begetter and that which he begat,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: [and] by the father and him whom he begot:

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: And by the giver of birth, and whom he fathered,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And [by] the father and that which was born [of him],

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: or by the great father and his wonderful son (Abraham and Ishmael)

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And the begetter and whom he begot.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: And (the mystic ties of) parent and child;-

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: That We created man in toil and trouble.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: We have indeed created man surrounded by hardships.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: indeed, We created man in trouble.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Assuredly We have created man in trouble.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Verily, We have created man in toil.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Verily We have created man into toil and hardship.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Verily, We have created man in Kabad.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: We verily have created man in an atmosphere:

4.author: Qarai
4.text: certainly We created man in travail.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: We created the human in fatigue.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: We have certainly created man into hardship.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: that We have created the human being to face a great deal of hardship.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Certainly We have created man to be in distress.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: that We have created man into a life of toil and trial.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Verily We have created man into toil and struggle.

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Does he think that no one has power over him?

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Does man think that no one will ever have power over him?

5.author: Arberry
5.text: What, does he think none has power over him,

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Deemest he that none will overpower him?

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Thinks he that none can overcome him?

5.author: Itani
5.text: Does he think that no one has power over him?

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Does he think that no one can overpower him?

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Does he think that none can overcome him

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Thinketh he that none hath power over him?

5.author: Qarai
5.text: Does he suppose that no one will ever have power over him?

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Does he think that none has power over him!

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Does he think that never will anyone overcome him?

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Does He think that no one will ever have control over him?

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Does he think that no one has power over him?

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Does he think then that no one has power over him?

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Thinketh he, that none hath power over him?

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Ayahsabu an lan yaqdira AAalayhi ahadun

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: He says: "I have wasted a great deal of wealth."

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: He saith: 'I have wasted riches plenteous.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: He says (boastfully): "I have wasted wealth in abundance!"

6.author: Itani
6.text: He says, “I have used up so much money.”

6.author: Maududi
6.text: He says: “I have squandered enormous wealth.”

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: He says: "I have wasted wealth in abundance!"

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And he saith: I have destroyed vast wealth:

6.author: Qarai
6.text: He says, ‘I have squandered immense wealth.’

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: He will say: 'I have destroyed a vast wealth'

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: He says, "I have spent wealth in abundance."

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: (He boasts and shows off) saying, "I have spent a great deal of money (for the cause of God)".

6.author: Shakir
6.text: He shall say: I have wasted much wealth.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: He says, "I have spent enormous wealth."

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: He may say (boastfully); Wealth have I squandered in abundance!

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Does he think that no one sees him?

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Does man think that no one saw him?

7.author: Arberry
7.text: What, does he think none has seen him?

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Deemest he that none beholdeth him?

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Thinks he that none sees him?

7.author: Itani
7.text: Does he think that no one sees him?

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Does he believe that none has seen him?

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Does he think that none sees him

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Thinketh he that none beholdeth him?

7.author: Qarai
7.text: Does he suppose that no one sees him?

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Does he think that none has observed him?

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Does he think that no one has seen him?

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Does he think that no one has seen him?

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Does he think that no one sees him?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Does he then think that no one sees him?

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Thinketh he that none beholdeth him?

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Did We not give him two eyes,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Did We not make two eyes for him?

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Have We not appointed to him two eyes,

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Have We not, made for him two eyes

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Have We not made for him a pair of eyes?

8.author: Itani
8.text: Did We not give him two eyes?

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Did We not grant him two eyes,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Have We not made for him two eyes

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Did We not assign unto him two eyes

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Have We not made for him two eyes,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Have We not given him two eyes,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Have We not made for him two eyes?

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Have We not given him two eyes,

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Have We not given him two eyes,

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Have We not given him two eyes,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Have We not made for him a pair of eyes?-

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And a tongue and two lips?

9.author: Arberry
9.text: and a tongue, and two lips,

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And a tongue and two lips,

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And a tongue and a pair of lips?

9.author: Itani
9.text: And a tongue, and two lips?

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and a tongue and two lips?

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And a tongue and two lips

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And a tongue and two lips,

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And a tongue and two lips?

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And a tongue and two lips,

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: and a tongue, and a pair of lips,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And a tongue, and a pair of lips?-

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: And showed him two highways (of good and evil)?

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And did We not guide him to the two elevated things?

10.author: Arberry
10.text: and guided him on the two highways?

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And shown Unto him the two highways?

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And shown him the two ways (good and evil)?

10.author: Itani
10.text: And We showed him the two ways?

10.author: Maududi
10.text: And did We not show him the two highroads (of good and evil)?

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And shown him the two ways

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And guide him to the parting of the mountain ways?

10.author: Qarai
10.text: and shown him the two paths [of good and evil]?

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: and guided him on the two paths (of good and evil)?

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And have shown him the two ways?

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Have We not shown him the ways of good and evil?

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And pointed out to him the two conspicuous ways?

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and shown him the two paths?

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: And shown him the two highways?

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: But he could not scale the steep ascent.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: So he did not quickly enter the steep valley.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Yet he has not assaulted the steep;

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Yet he attempteth not the steep,

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: But he has made no effort to pass on the path that is steep.

11.author: Itani
11.text: But he did not brave the ascent.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: But he did not venture to scale the difficult steep.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: But he has not attempted to pass on the path that is steep.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: But he hath not attempted the Ascent -

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Yet he has not embarked upon the uphill task.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Yet he has not scaled the height.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: But he has not broken through the difficult pass.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: Yet, he has not entered into Aqaba

11.author: Shakir
11.text: But he would not attempt the uphill road,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: But he has not attempted the ascent.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: But he hath made no haste on the path that is steep.

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: How will you comprehend what the Steep ascent is? --

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And what have you understood, what the valley is!

12.author: Arberry
12.text: and what shall teach thee what is the steep?

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And what shall make thee understand that which the steep is?

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And what will make you know the path that is steep?

12.author: Itani
12.text: And what will explain to you what the ascent is?

12.author: Maududi
12.text: And what do you know what that difficult steep is?

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And what will make you know the path that is steep

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what the Ascent is! -

12.author: Qarai
12.text: And what will show you what is the uphill task?

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: What could let you know what the height is!

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And what can make you know what is [breaking through] the difficult pass?

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: Would that you knew what Aqaba is!

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And what will make you comprehend what the uphill road is?

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: What will explain to you what the ascent is?

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: And what will explain to thee the path that is steep?-

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: To free a neck (from the burden of debt or slavery),

13.author: Maududi
13.text: It is freeing someone's neck from slavery;

13.author: Qarai
13.text: [It is] the freeing of a slave,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: (It is) the freeing of a slave,

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: It is the freeing of a slave

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: It is the setting free of a slave

13.author: Shakir
13.text: (It is) the setting free of a slave,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: It is the freeing of a slave;

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Or to feed in times of famine

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Or the feeding on a day of hunger.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: or giving food upon a day of hunger

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Or, feeding, in a day of privation,

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Or giving food in a day of hunger (famine),

14.author: Itani
14.text: Or the feeding on a day of hunger.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: or giving food on a day of hunger

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Or giving food in a day full of Masghabah,

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And to feed in the day of hunger.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: or feeding, during days of [general] starvation,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: the giving of food upon the day of hunger

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: Or feeding on a day of severe hunger

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: or, in a day of famine, the feeding of

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Or the giving of food in a day of hunger

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: or the feeding in times of famine

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Or the giving of food in a day of privation

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Aw itAAamun fee yawmin thee masghabatin

15.author: Arberry
15.text: to an orphan near of kin

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: To an orphan near of kin.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: to an orphan near of kin;

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: To an orphan near of kin.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: To the orphan with claims of relationship,

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: Or of a homeless needy person!

16.author: Arberry
16.text: or a needy man in misery;

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: Or a poor man cleaving to the dust.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Or to a Miskin (poor) afflicted with misery.

16.author: Itani
16.text: Or a destitute in the dust.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: or to a destitute lying in dust;

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Or to a Miskin cleaving to dust.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Or some poor wretch in misery,

16.author: Qarai
16.text: or a needy man in desolation,

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: or to a needy person in distress;

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: Or a needy person in misery

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: and downtrodden destitute person, (so that he would be of)

16.author: Shakir
16.text: Or to the poor man lying in the dust.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: or some needy person in distress,

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Or to the indigent (down) in the dust.

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: And to be of those who believe, and urge upon one another to persevere, and urge upon each other to be kind.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And moreover to be of those who accepted faith, and who urged patience to one another and who urged graciousness to one another.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: then that he become of those who believe and counsel each other to be steadfast, and counsel each other to be merciful.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Then he became of those who believed and enjoined on each other steadfastness and enjoined on each other compassion.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Then he became one of those who believed, and recommended one another to perseverance and patience, and (also) recommended one another to pity and compassion.

17.author: Itani
17.text: Then he becomes of those who believe, and advise one another to patience, and advise one another to kindness.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: and, then besides this, he be one of those who believed, and enjoined upon one another steadfastness and enjoined upon one another compassion.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Then he became one of those who believed and recommended one another to patience, and recommended one another to compassion.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And to be of those who believe and exhort one another to perseverance and exhort one another to pity.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: while being one of those who have faith and enjoin one another to patience, and enjoin one another to compassion.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: so that he becomes one of those who believe, charge each other to be patient, and charge each other to be merciful.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And then being among those who believed and advised one another to patience and advised one another to compassion.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: the believers who cooperate with others in patience (steadfastness) and kindness.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Then he is of those who believe and charge one another to show patience, and charge one another to show compassion.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and to be one of those who believe and urge one another to steadfastness and compassion.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Then will he be of those who believe, and enjoin patience, (constancy, and self-restraint), and enjoin deeds of kindness and compassion.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Thumma kana mina allatheena amanoo watawasaw bialssabri watawasaw bialmarhamati

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: They are the people of the right hand (and will succeed).

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: These are the people of the right.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: Those are the Companions of the Right Hand.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: These are the fellows of the righthand.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: They are those on the Right Hand (the dwellers of Paradise),

18.author: Itani
18.text: These are the people of happiness.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: These are the People of the Right Hand.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: They are those on the Right,

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Their place will be on the right hand.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: They are the People of the Right Hand.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Those are the Companions of the Right.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Those are the companions of the right.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: These are the people of the right hand.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: These are the people of the right hand.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: Those who do so are the people of the right hand,

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Such are the Companions of the Right Hand.

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: But those who deny Our revelations are the people of the left hand:

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And those who denied Our signs, are the people of the left.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: And those who disbelieve in Our signs, they are the Companions of the Left Hand;

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And those who disbelieve Our signs - they are the fellows of the lefthand.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: But those who disbelieved in Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), they are those on the Left Hand (the dwellers of Hell).

19.author: Itani
19.text: But as for those who defy Our revelations—these are the people of misery.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: As for those who rejected Our Signs, they are the People of the Left Hand.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: But those who disbelieved in Our Ayat, they are those on the Left.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: But those who disbelieve Our revelations, their place will be on the left hand.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: But those who defy Our signs, they are the People of the Left Hand.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: But those who disbelieve in Our verses, they are the Companions of the Left,

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: But they who disbelieved in Our signs - those are the companions of the left.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: As for those who disbelieve in Our revelations, they are the people of the left

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And (as for) those who disbelieve in our communications, they are the people of the left hand.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and [as for] those who are bent on denying the truth of Our revelations, they are the people of the left hand,

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: But those who reject Our Signs, they are the (unhappy) Companions of the Left Hand.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Waallatheena kafaroo biayatina hum ashabu almashamati

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: The Fire will vault them over.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Upon them is a fire, in which they are imprisoned, closed and shut above them.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: over them is a Fire covered down.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Over them shall be Fire closing round.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: The Fire will be shut over them (i.e. they will be enveloped by the Fire without any opening or window or outlet.

20.author: Itani
20.text: Upon them is a padlocked Fire.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Upon them shall be a Fire that will hem them in.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Fire will be an awning over them.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: A closed Fire will be [imposed] upon them.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: with the Fire closed above them.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Over them will be fire closed in.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: who will be engulfed in the fire.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: On them is fire closed over.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: and the Fire will close in on them.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: On them will be Fire vaulted over (all round).
